title: Lausanne-based Rhonexum raises â¬867.5k to advance cryogenic electronics for scalable quantum computing | EU-Startups
why:
- Rhonexum has raised €867.5k in pre-seed funding to accelerate product development and begin commercialising its first cryogenic electronics product for scalable quantum computing.
- The company develops electronics that operate at cryogenic temperatures close to absolute zero (-270°C), which are essential for quantum computing, overcoming the limitations of conventional electronics in such environments.
- Rhonexum's technology enables electronics to operate directly within cryogenic systems, removing a major scalability bottleneck by allowing electronics to function close to the qubits.
- The startup uses proprietary models and software-driven simulations for cryogenic conditions, allowing faster and more cost-effective hardware development.
- The company's first industrial-grade cryogenic electronics product is scheduled for delivery to initial customers later this year.
